picks.reason: deGrom's elite AGG xFIP/SIERA edge over Pérez is offset by road factors, slight platoon edge, and TEX bullpen availability issues, producing an estimated TEX win probability of ~56% against the best-ML implied ~54.8% for -121. Gap falls below the 4-point threshold. Projected total run gap vs 7.5 is under 0.75 runs after park/weather/bullpen adjustments. No side qualifies, so totals ignored.

Bibee holds a clear AGG xFIP advantage over poor Fedde metrics, but CLE's weak vs-RHP platoon wRC+ and road status limit the edge to an estimated 54-55% CLE win probability vs -118 implied ~54.1%. Gap under 4 points. No side bet qualifies to allow totals consideration; run projection gap also marginal.

Suarez's moderate xFIP edge over Freeland is overwhelmed by Coors' massive 114/130 run inflation and home-team variance, yielding an estimated BOS win probability around 50-52% against -165 implied ~62.3%. Negative or negligible gap. High-variance park makes any total edge unreliable without a qualifying side.

Soriano's solid AGG metrics hold a large edge over Gibson's small-sample poor numbers, but the [sm] flag on the away starter caps any potential at 1 unit and the estimated LAA win probability reaches only ~57% vs -125 implied 55.6%. Gap below 4 points. No qualifying side, so totals passed.

Nola's AGG xFIP advantage over Mikolas is modest after [sm] L14 adjustment and platoon considerations, producing an estimated PHI win probability of ~55-56% vs -133 implied ~57.1%. Insufficient gap. No side bet, so no totals.

Lowder's poor AGG xFIP gives MIL a pitching edge despite both [sm] L14 samples, but road factors yield an estimated MIL win probability of ~56-57% vs -144 implied ~59%. Gap under threshold. No side, totals ignored.

Ohtani and Ryan post comparable AGG xFIP with Ohtani's Stf+ edge offset by road platoon and bullpen factors, producing an estimated LAD win probability of ~57-59% against heavy -180 implied ~64.3%. Negative gap. No side qualifies for totals.
